references.name: Swing tempo
references.rationale: A repeatable backswing-to-downswing sequence supports predictable timing.
references.correction_cue: Use a smooth takeaway and let the transition begin before accelerating through impact.
references.source_title: Tour Tempo - Golf's Last Secret Finally Revealed
references.source_note: The commonly taught 3:1 full-swing tempo relationship informs this operational tolerance band; it is not a performance score.

references.name: Lateral head stability
references.rationale: Excess lateral head travel can make low-point control less repeatable.
references.correction_cue: Keep your chest turning while your head remains centred over the address area.
references.source_note: TPI defines excessive lower-body lateral movement in the backswing; the threshold here is an initial pose-analysis calibration expressed in shoulder widths.

references.name: Hip sway
references.rationale: Large lateral hip motion during the swing reduces the room available to rotate consistently.
references.correction_cue: Feel the trail hip turn behind you rather than shifting away from the target.
references.source_note: The threshold operationalises TPI's sway characteristic for 2D pose review and should be calibrated with labelled swings.

references.name: Lead-arm extension at the top
references.rationale: A substantially collapsed lead arm changes swing radius and can reduce repeatability.
references.correction_cue: Maintain width by allowing your lead arm to extend as your shoulders complete the turn.
references.source_title: TPI - 5 Key Fundamentals of a Great Golf Swing
references.source_note: TPI discusses straight lead-arm characteristics; the angle band is an initial computer-vision proxy rather than a required model position.

references.name: Finish stability
references.rationale: A controlled finish is a useful observable proxy for balance after the strike.
references.correction_cue: Swing through to a balanced finish that you can hold without stepping or leaning.
references.source_title: TPI - 5 Key Fundamentals of a Great Golf Swing
references.source_note: This is a pose-based balance proxy; it does not assess strike quality.

references.name: Address spine inclination
references.rationale: Athletic hip hinge provides room to rotate while retaining posture.
references.correction_cue: Set your posture from the hips with a long spine and relaxed arms below the shoulders.
references.source_title: Titleist Performance Institute - Loss of Posture
references.source_note: The club-family address range is an initial operational threshold for down-the-line pose data.

references.name: Address spine inclination
references.rationale: Athletic hip hinge provides room to rotate while retaining posture.
references.correction_cue: Set your posture from the hips with a long spine and relaxed arms below the shoulders.
references.source_title: Titleist Performance Institute - Loss of Posture
references.source_note: The club-family address range is an initial operational threshold for down-the-line pose data.

references.name: Address knee flex
references.rationale: An athletic address posture supports rotation without excessive vertical movement.
references.correction_cue: Begin in an athletic stance with soft knee flex rather than sitting deeply or locking the legs.
references.source_title: Titleist Performance Institute - Loss of Posture
references.source_note: The angle band is a pose-review baseline and not an exact prescription for every golfer.

references.name: Posture retention
references.rationale: Large posture change in the downswing can reduce space for the hands and make contact harder to repeat.
references.correction_cue: Maintain your hip hinge as you turn through the downswing rather than standing up early.
references.source_title: Titleist Performance Institute - Loss of Posture
references.source_note: This threshold is a starting proxy for the TPI characteristic using shoulder-to-hip inclination.

references.name: Head stability
references.rationale: Large visible motion can indicate difficulty retaining address posture.
references.correction_cue: Keep your posture while rotating so the upper body does not move abruptly toward or away from the ball.
references.source_title: Titleist Performance Institute - Loss of Posture
references.source_note: This check is limited to visible 2D motion and does not diagnose a cause.
